title: Error: creating build container: copying system image from manifest list: parsing image configuration
body:
### Issue Description

Failed to build `docker.io/volkerraschek/helm` from [source](https://github.com/volker-raschek/helm-docker). Get the following error message: *Error: creating build container: copying system image from manifest list: parsing image configuration: (heuristic tuning data: last retry 0, current offset 0; 0.015 ms total, NaN ms since progress): unexpected EOF*



### Steps to reproduce the issue

```bash
$ git clone https://github.com/volker-raschek/helm-docker.git /tmp/helm-docker && make -C /tmp/helm-docker container-image/build
Klone nach '/tmp/helm-docker'...
remote: Enumerating objects: 161, done.
remote: Counting objects: 100% (37/37), done.
remote: Compressing objects: 100% (28/28), done.
remote: Total 161 (delta 16), reused 27 (delta 9), pack-reused 124
Empfange Objekte: 100% (161/161), 29.87 KiB | 728.00 KiB/s, fertig.
Löse Unterschiede auf: 100% (85/85), fertig.
make: Verzeichnis „/tmp/helm-docker“ wird betreten
/usr/bin/podman build \
        --build-arg HELM_VERSION=v3.11.3  \
        --file Dockerfile \
        --no-cache \
        --pull \
        --tag docker.io/volkerraschek/helm:latest \
        --tag volkerraschek/helm:latest \
        .
STEP 1/8: FROM docker.io/library/alpine:3.17
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:3.17...
Error: creating build container: copying system image from manifest list: parsing image configuration: (heuristic tuning data: last retry 0, current offset 0; 0.080 ms total, NaN ms since progress): unexpected EOF
```


### Describe the results you received

Failed to build the container image

### Describe the results you expected

A successful build of the container image via podman.

### podman info output

```yaml

      commit: 59f2beb7efb0d35611d5818fd0311883676f6f7e
      rundir: /run/user/1000/crun
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL

      libseccomp: 2.5.4

  graphRootUsed: 489498882048
  graphStatus:
    Build Version: Btrfs v6.2.2
    Library Version: "102"
  imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp
  imageStore:
    number: 2
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  transientStore: false
  volumePath: /home/markus/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 4.4.4
  Built: 1680009696
  BuiltTime: Tue Mar 28 15:21:36 2023
  GitCommit: c8223435f49a860c8ac4281a85db39232745a7cb-dirty
  GoVersion: go1.20.2
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 4.4.4
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details: 
- Configured a registry mirror for docker.io. Use container image docker.io/library/registry as local mirror. 
- Use btrfs as storage driver

### Additional information

Maybe it has something to do with the following [project](https://github.com/multiarch/qemu-user-static) and the command which I executed the last days to test how I can cross-build container images on amd64 based architectures: `sudo podman run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes`. The command is documented in the README of the project. Possibly the command with `sudo` and `--privileged` reconfigured my host system, which is why I get the error message. Unfortunately, I could not find anything about the error message, so I am not able to find out if the host system was reconfigured or not - which may lead to the error.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @volker-raschek.

Are you running behind a proxy?  Can you try without the local mirror?  Does it work when doing a simple `{sudo} podman pull docker.io/library/alpine:3.17`?

FWIW, I cannot reproduce on my machine.

Hi @vrothberg, 
no I am not behind a proxy.  

_Can you try without the local mirror?_

I disabled the mirror configuration in `~/.config/containers/registries.conf.d/default.conf`. The download of the image was successful afterwards. The container image `docker.io/volkerraschek/helm` could also be built without mirror configuration. 

```bash
$ podman pull docker.io/library/alpine:3.17
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:3.17...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob f56be85fc22e done  
Copying config 9ed4aefc74 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
9ed4aefc74f6792b5a804d1d146fe4b4a2299147b0f50eaf2b08435d7b38c27e
```

I could have come up with the idea of deactivating the mirror. Many thanks for this already. What surprises me, however, is that it comes from overnight to the error. I have been using the local mirror for several months. 

_Does it work when doing a simple_ `{sudo} podman pull docker.io/library/alpine:3.17`

Yes this works out of the box, because there is no mirror configured for root.

Here are the logs of the local mirror when I tried to build the image with mirror configuration:

```
10.244.1.118 - - [13/Apr/2023:09:39:47 +0000] "GET /v2/ HTTP/1.1" 200 2 "" "Buildah/1.29.0"
time="2023-04-13T09:39:47.233405333Z" level=info msg="response completed" go.version=go1.16.15 http.request.host=dhcm.example.local http.request.id=22fb8ed9-ca11-46df-93f6-9b0968f9f873 http.request.method=GET http.request.remoteaddr=10.244.0.0 http.request.uri="/v2/" http.request.useragent="Buildah/1.29.0" http.response.contenttype="application/json; charset=utf-8" http.response.duration=2.326812ms http.response.status=200 http.response.written=2 
time="2023-04-13T09:39:48.230530885Z" level=info msg="response completed" go.version=go1.16.15 http.request.host=dhcm.example.local http.request.id=a4ce4a43-0d92-4e02-bb32-a24b2ab5e1e0 http.request.method=GET http.request.remoteaddr=10.244.0.0 http.request.uri="/v2/library/alpine/manifests/3.17" http.request.useragent="Buildah/1.29.0" http.response.contenttype="application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json" http.response.duration=979.096723ms http.response.status=200 http.response.written=1638 
10.244.1.118 - - [13/Apr/2023:09:39:47 +0000] "GET /v2/library/alpine/manifests/3.17 HTTP/1.1" 200 1638 "" "Buildah/1.29.0"
time="2023-04-13T09:39:48.268412288Z" level=info msg="response completed" go.version=go1.16.15 http.request.host=dhcm.example.local http.request.id=e1886800-8779-4606-82a3-d1993d542852 http.request.method=GET http.request.remoteaddr=10.244.0.0 http.request.uri="/v2/library/alpine/manifests/sha256:b6ca290b6b4cdcca5b3db3ffa338ee0285c11744b4a6abaa9627746ee3291d8d" http.request.useragent="Buildah/1.29.0" http.response.contenttype="application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json" http.response.duration=15.20009ms http.response.status=200 http.response.written=528 
10.244.1.118 - - [13/Apr/2023:09:39:48 +0000] "GET /v2/library/alpine/manifests/sha256:b6ca290b6b4cdcca5b3db3ffa338ee0285c11744b4a6abaa9627746ee3291d8d HTTP/1.1" 200 528 "" "Buildah/1.29.0"
time="2023-04-13T09:39:48.310442869Z" level=info msg="response completed" go.version=go1.16.15 http.request.host=dhcm.example.local http.request.id=fd182ea1-67f6-42f5-afcd-dfd069522c33 http.request.method=GET http.request.remoteaddr=10.244.0.0 http.request.uri="/v2/library/alpine/blobs/sha256:9ed4aefc74f6792b5a804d1d146fe4b4a2299147b0f50eaf2b08435d7b38c27e" http.request.useragent="Buildah/1.29.0" http.response.contenttype="application/octet-stream" http.response.duration=24.85381ms http.response.status=200 http.response.written=0 
10.244.1.118 - - [13/Apr/2023:09:39:48 +0000] "GET /v2/library/alpine/blobs/sha256:9ed4aefc74f6792b5a804d1d146fe4b4a2299147b0f50eaf2b08435d7b38c27e HTTP/1.1" 200 0 "" "Buildah/1.29.0"
```

I can manually delete the image `docker.io/library/alpine:3.17` from the mirror and test it again if no any further steps should be tested. Maybe the image is broken?

I use the container image `docker.io/library/registry:2.8.1` as mirror server.

Thanks for checking, @volker-raschek !

> Here are the logs of the local mirror when I tried to build the image with mirror configuration:

I do not see anything suspicious in the logs.

> What surprises me, however, is that it comes from overnight to the error. I have been using the local mirror for several months.

It could be a bug in `registry:2.8.1`.  Can you pull other images from the mirror or is it only this specific one?

> I can manually delete the image docker.io/library/alpine:3.17 from the mirror and test it again if no any further steps should be tested. Maybe the image is broken?

Definitely worth a try.  But I really cannot be certain since I am not familiar with the internals of `registry:2`.

Hi @vrothberg,

_Can you pull other images from the mirror or is it only this specific one?_

It seems to be only affect the image `docker.io/library/alpine:3.17`

```bash
$ podman pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:20.04
Trying to pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:20.04...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 06d39c85623a done  
Copying config 1c5c8d0b97 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
1c5c8d0b973ab9d6d7742cf050beefa8ba2efb7779e09bfb1200d6192180766e

$ podman pull docker.io/library/alpine:3.17
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:3.17...
Error: copying system image from manifest list: parsing image configuration: (heuristic tuning data: last retry 0, current offset 0; 0.543 ms total, NaN ms since progress): unexpected EOF
```

I deleted the directory `registry/v2/repositories/library/alpine` of the mirror and restarted the application. I can now pull the container image and build `docker.io/volkerraschek/helm` via the mirror configuration successfully. 

This hardens my suspicion that the image on the local mirror was broken.

I think at this point it is no longer a bug in podman and the issue can be closed.

Thank you for your help

  Markus
Awesome, glad we could resolve the issue. Thanks for reaching out!
